qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated Jetty admin HTTP server exposes all JVM system properties, server configuration, and client connection details
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets unauthenticated Jetty admin CommandServlet endpoints that return JVM/server state; the advisory targets ZKConfig improperly logging sensitive client configuration values to the client's logfile.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Deterministic session password generation uses java.util.Random with hardcoded seed enabling session hijacking
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets deterministic ZooKeeper session password derivation in ZooKeeperServer.generatePasswd/reopenSession; the advisory targets ZKConfig logging of sensitive client configuration values.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-crhr-qqj8-rpxc/CVE-2026-24308 is about improper handling of configuration values in ZKConfig causing sensitive client configuration values to be written to the client's logfile at INFO level. The qualifying findings instead cover unauthenticated Jetty admin endpoints and deterministic session password generation.
evidence: Advisory details name ZKConfig and CWE-532-style exposure of sensitive client configuration values in client logfiles.
unauthenticated-admin-info-disclosure reports JettyAdminServer.CommandServlet endpoints such as /commands/system_properties and /commands/configuration, not ZKConfig logging.
weak-prng-session-password reports ZooKeeperServer.generatePasswd/reopenSession deterministic java.util.Random session password derivation, not configuration value logging.
